free_text: Implications for statistical theories of fragmentation
from measurements of Ag(p,3He) and Ag(p,4He) at
intermediate energies
Natural silver targets of mean thickness 2.3 mg/cm2
mounted in aluminum frame.
Absolute normalization of the data was obtained by
relating the counting rate for 40 to 80 MeV 4He
fragments in a monitor counter at 90 degrees to the
counting rate from proton elastic scattering in a
polarimeter mounted in the proton beam line
approximately 1.5 m beyond the target.
Correction made for light mass impurities on the Ag
target using approximately 100 micro-g/cm2 VYNS
film.
Absolute normalizations should be accurate to +-25%.
Relative normalizations for angular distributions
should be accurate to 5%.
Tabulated data received from R. Korteling, September
Converted to new date formats, lower case.
BIB update.
